title: Key Derivation, HKDF, and Key Separation · Language Lens: Crypto Go Kdf Version Migration
summary: A protocol slices one shared secret into keys, uses user passwords as HKDF input, omits transcript context, and changes output length without versioning. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the kdf version migration increment in labeled key schedule and derivation tree. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Version derivation labels and parameters, preserve old read paths, prevent downgrade, and prove rotation rollback.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Kdf Version Migration · Theory Crypto Go Kdf Version Migration
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 2684 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive bounded purpose- and role-separated HKDF keys bound to the negotiated transcript; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
